## What does this PR do?

<!-- Brief description. Which module(s) or files are added/changed? -->

## Type of change

- [ ] New module
- [ ] Module correction (factual fix — include a source below)
- [ ] Docs / examples / rubric
- [ ] Tooling (`tools/`)

## Sources (for any factual claim about history, language, or scholarship)

<!-- List references, or write "n/a — no new factual claims". -->

## Contributor checklist

- [ ] `python tools/validate.py framework/` passes locally.
- [ ] Frontmatter is complete; `id` matches `<type>.<slug>` and the folder.
- [ ] All required body sections are present and in order.
- [ ] Factual claims are **sourced** or **explicitly framed as uncertain** — no invented authorities.
- [ ] Divided questions present the major views fairly, labeled (consensus / majority / minority / speculative).
- [ ] **Neutrality self-check:** this module describes *method*; it does **not** tell the reader what to believe.
- [ ] `requires` / `recommends` reference real module ids; `[[id]]` cross-references resolve.

## Reviewer note

New modules require **2 maintainer approvals**, one being an explicit
**neutrality + sourcing** review (see GOVERNANCE.md §3).
